source.roadArticle: San Juan Skyway Scenic and Historic Byway

attribution.text: Route, junction, and history details adapted from the Wikipedia article "San Juan Skyway Scenic and Historic Byway", by Wikipedia contributors, licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0.

sources.name: Federal Highway Administration — National Scenic Byways

paragraphs: The San Juan Skyway was designated as a National Forest Scenic Byway in September 1988. It was later named a Colorado Scenic and Historic Byway in 1989 and an All-American Road in September 1996.

references.text: "San Juan Skyway". Colorado Department of Transportation. 2021. Retrieved April 8, 2022.
"San Juan Skyway". America's Scenic Byways. Retrieved April 8, 2022.
Staff. "San Juan Skyway". National Scenic Byways. Federal Highway Administration. Retrieved September 11, 2011.
Staff. "Official Designations". San Juan Skyway. Federal Highway Administration. Retrieved September 11, 2011.

gallery.cap: National Scenic Byway San Juan Skyway (Colorado)
San Juan Skyway Bear Creek Signs
gallery.desc: These interpretive signs are located at Bear Creek, on the western leg of the Byway near Telluride. Location: Telluride, Colorado (37.921° N 107.806° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Sally Pearce

gallery.cap: San Juan Skyway Hardships and Healthcare Room in Telluride Historical
gallery.desc: A group of first graders learn more about healthcare in historic Telluride in the "Hardships and Healthcare Room." Location: Telluride, Colorado (37.938° N 107.814° W) Status: Public domain. Photo courtesy of the Telluride Historical Museum

gallery.cap: San Juan Skyway Origins Room in Telluride Historical Museum
gallery.desc: The "Origins Room" tells the extraordinary history of the Ute Indians in the San Miguel valley. Location: Telluride, Colorado (37.938° N 107.813° W) Status: Public domain. Photo courtesy of the Telluride Historical Museum

gallery.cap: San Juan Skyway Telluride Historical Museum
gallery.desc: The Telluride Historical Museum looks charmingly historic and pristine after its complete restoration. Location: Telluride, Colorado (37.938° N 107.812° W) Status: Public domain. Photo courtesy of the Telluride Historical Museum
Uncompahgre Gorge and Mount Abrams between Silverton, Colorado and Ouray, Colorado on Highway 550
View of San Juan Mountains from San Juan Skyway

One more colorful valley on San Juan Skyway. If you haven't seen it by yourself you can hardly believe it's real. Deutsche Beschreibung Ein weiteres farbenfrohes Tal am San Juan Skyway. Wenn Du es nicht mal selber gesehen hast, kann man kaum glauben, dass es Realität ist.
This interpretive site allows the driver to rest and focus on the view. Location: Colorado (37.942° N 107.810° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Dennis Adams

gallery.cap: Autumn on the Red Mountain Pass
gallery.desc: Golden aspens grace the roadside on the Red Mountain Pass on a still autumn day. Location: Red Mountain Pass, Colorado (37.923° N 107.697° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Sally Pearce

gallery.cap: Backdrop of the San Juan Mountains
gallery.desc: The San Juan Mountains provide a majestic backdrop to the valley below. Location: Colorado (37.449° N 107.804° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by K. Armstrong
gallery.credit: K. Armstrong, U.S. Department of Transportation · Public domain

gallery.desc: A wooden bridge crosses Bear Creek near Telluride. Location: Telluride, Colorado (37.927° N 107.805° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Sally Pearce
An SUV takes to the dirt roads of the San Juan Skyway to catch a view of autumn leaves in the distance. Location: Colorado (37.966° N 107.585° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Dennis Adams

gallery.cap: Dirt Roads of the Alpine Loop
gallery.desc: Explore the Alpine Loop by taking this dirt road. Location: Colorado (37.979° N 107.499° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Dennis Adams
Surrounded by mountains and pasture, Telluride is a nice getaway spot. Location: Telluride, Colorado (37.950° N 107.871° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Dennis Adams
Visitors stop by an informative kiosk at the Durango Visitor Center along the San Juan Skyway. Location: Durango, Colorado (37.269° N 107.885° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Dennis Adams
